name: Energy Sector (Global)
summary: Day 63 Thursday. ENERGY SECTOR LEADERSHIP RESUMES — XLE +2.3% 1D ON SECTOR ROTATION INTO OIL-LEVERAGED NAMES; CHENIERE +2.7%, EXXON +2.7%, CF +3.7%. (1) XLE $59.03 (+2.3% 1D, -4.7% 1M, +16.4% 3M) — sector rotation into oil-leveraged equities catching up to Brent strength; first session where the prior-month underperformance is reversing. (2) Cheniere LNG $272.23 (+2.7% 1D, -7.3% 1M, +29.0% 3M) — TTF stable €44.13 + US LNG export-arb durable + Qatar 17% offline persists. (3) Exxon Mobil $154.67 (+2.7% 1D, -9.8% 1M, +10.1% 3M) — Brent realization on US exports + Permian production; refinery margins from Heating Oil +4.5% / RBOB +1.2%. (4) CF Industries $126.78 (+3.7% 1D, -7.9% 1M, +36.7% 3M) — urea/fertilizer firming on Qatar capacity offline + Brent feedstock cost-pass-through. US energy sector is the cleanest beneficiary trade — pays WTI, sells Brent, exports LNG at TTF premium. For deployment thesis: US energy was a 'buy on Hormuz Day 60+' trade through Apr; if today extends to a sustained 5%+ rally, the entry window narrows. COUNTERWEIGHT: Lockheed -0.5% / ITA -1.4%; ITA +1.1% 1M but LMT -14.8% 1M — 'war helps defense' narrative is empirically WRONG for Q1 because defense companies need actual contracts and execution, not just headlines. INVESTOR PAIR TRADE: LONG energy / SHORT defense has been the alpha generator over April; continues to work today. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
